message:
chore(beads): file flywheel_connectors-0cncv (fcp-bootstrap cert validity clock-skew tolerance)

One new open bead tracking a hardening follow-up on the X.509
certificate chain validation path in fcp-bootstrap (the same area of
code where 3ff09df4 pinned BasicConstraints and 00c8943f added
keyCertSign enforcement).

The bead — `[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks
clock-skew tolerance` — captures the observation that
`x509_parser::Validity::is_valid()` checks notBefore/notAfter against
wallclock `now` with zero tolerance, so a provisioning attempt during
a small clock drift window on either the host or the token issuer
will reject an otherwise-valid chain.

No other beads are modified; no code changes in this commit.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","title":"[FCP3/P7.4] Move or delete the remaining non-primitive semantics from fcp-core","description":"Background: once the remaining ownership map is explicit, the broad-bucket pattern only disappears if the repo actually moves or deletes the stray semantics.

Scope: relocate or delete the remaining semantic surfaces that do not belong in `fcp-core`, tighten exports, and leave only intentionally minimal shared primitives behind.

Acceptance: the surviving `fcp-core` footprint is intentionally narrow, and the removed meanings are now owned by better crates without dropping user-visible behavior or diagnostic fidelity.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.255208Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:21.227649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-core","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.255208Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:09.353205Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2435,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group retirement note: this track retires semantic ownership from `fcp-core`, not just imports. Require nearby unit coverage for moved semantics, integration or conformance proof where behavior crosses crates, and crate-graph or import-surface artifacts that make the post-retirement ownership boundary mechanically visible.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","title":"[FCP3/P7.4] Publish the crate-graph and import-surface proof for fcp-core retirement","description":"Background: semantic retirement needs proof at the crate graph and import-surface level so future contributors can see that ownership blur is actually gone.

Scope: capture the final crate-graph audit, import-surface audit, and regression evidence for `fcp-core` retirement. Show what imports disappeared, what moved, and which tests and artifacts prove the new ownership shape is real.

Acceptance: the repo has concrete audit artifacts that prove `fcp-core` is no longer acting as a semantic junk drawer.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.941660Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:21.645250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-core","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7","proof","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.941660Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:10.106313Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2436,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group retirement note: this track retires semantic ownership from `fcp-core`, not just imports. Require nearby unit coverage for moved semantics, integration or conformance proof where behavior crosses crates, and crate-graph or import-surface artifacts that make the post-retirement ownership boundary mechanically visible.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","title":"fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status","description":"# fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to track envelope progress:
- delivered/signed/completed/declined/voided
- recipient-level status (without leaking PII)

## Scope
- Get envelope status.
- List envelopes with filters (date range, status).
- Optional: ingest Connect webhooks for real-time status updates.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.envelopes.read`
- `docusign.webhook.ingest` (optional)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - recipient names/emails are sensitive
  - logs include only envelope_id + status + timestamps

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.envelope.status.get`
  - `docusign.envelope.list`
  - Optional: `docusign.webhook.ingest`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - status parsing
  - webhook replay/idempotency (if enabled)

## Acceptance criteria
- Envelope status tracking is reliable and safe for automation.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:33.859686294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:54.942469Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T20:00:23.184885Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bijm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bijm.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1372,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Already fully covered by existing operations: docusign.get_envelope (status.get) and docusign.list_envelopes (envelope.list). Both operations return full status information including recipient status. No additional work needed.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:00:23Z"},{"id":1381,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented tracking.rs: EnvelopeStatusInfo with progress tracking, RecipientStatus (privacy-safe: no email/name), WebhookEventType (8 types), EnvelopeListQuery builder. 149 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:54Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cncv","title":"[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks clock-skew tolerance","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1223 and :1249 both call parsed.validity().is_valid() to check X.509 NotBefore/NotAfter against system time, with no skew tolerance. A machine with clock drift (cold-boot before NTP sync, VM clock, etc.) can silently reject freshly-provisioned certificates whose NotBefore is seconds in the future from the host's perspective, producing a cryptic NoVerifiedIssuerChain error with no hint that it's a clock issue.

Standard PKI practice is to accept certs within a small skew window (±60s typical).

Fix: add a helper cert_valid_with_skew(&parsed, 60_secs) that uses parsed.validity().not_before / .not_after and OffsetDateTime::now_utc() directly, accepting a forward-skew on NotBefore. Keep NotAfter strict (never accept expired). Apply at both call sites.

Not a vulnerability — defensive hardening for cold-bootstrap robustness on clock-skewed machines.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","title":"[fcp-e2e] Enable kill_on_drop for connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs claimed to avoid zombie leaks in Drop, but it only called Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Switch the spawned command to kill_on_drop(true), remove the misleading manual Drop path, and add regression coverage that a dropped long-running runner no longer leaves its PID present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:41.568206Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422700Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422177Z","close_reason":"Stale-complete: kill_on_drop implementation and regression coverage already present in worktree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Verified current worktree state for this bead.

Findings:
- `crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` already sets `Command::kill_on_drop(true)` in `ConnectorProcessRunner::spawn()`.
- The old manual-Drop approach is no longer present.
- A Unix regression test exists at `drop_reaps_long_running_process`, which checks that dropping a long-running runner makes its PID disappear.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-e2e --all-targets` passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt -p fcp-e2e -- --check` passed.
- `ubs crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` is not trustworthy for this single Rust file in this repo state; UBS misclassified the path across multiple language scanners and emitted obvious false positives like JavaScript loose-equality warnings on Rust syntax.
- A targeted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib drop_reaps_long_running_process -- --nocapture` did not reach the `fcp-e2e` test body because the remote build hit an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/hpke.rs` on the worker, despite the local worktree already containing a fix for that line.
- Earlier remote test runs also hit separate `rch` artifact-retrieval failures caused by pathological nested paths under `.beads/recovery_*`; that is operational fallout, not evidence against this bead implementation.

Conclusion: the requested `kill_on_drop` change and regression coverage are already present in the current worktree, so this bead is stale-complete from a backlog perspective. Remaining noise is in unrelated verification infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","title":"[FCP3/P4.3] Unify runtime state, error mapping, and health and checkpoint adapters","description":"Background: even with shared kernel and SDK contracts, runtime state and failure handling can drift if error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapters are implemented separately by host, SDK, and connector families.

Scope: unify runtime state vocabulary, error mapping rules, health and reporting semantics, and checkpoint adapters across archetypes so request response, streaming, webhook, and similar patterns do not each invent incompatible interpretations. Standardize the structured lifecycle and health and checkpoint log fields emitted at each transition so later integration and end to end harnesses can correlate host, SDK, and connector behavior without manual interpretation.

Acceptance: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations, and the same vocabulary powers diagnostics and regression tests instead of spawning parallel interpretations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.439780Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42.148877Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:50.136219Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","health","mesh-first","phase-4","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.589697Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.073860Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2213,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Claiming after closing blocker tbjdk (P4.1). Starting with code archaeology: mapping runtime state vocabulary across fcp-host, fcp-sdk, fcp-kernel, and connector archetypes to identify divergent error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapter patterns. Goal: one vocabulary for runtime behavior that powers diagnostics and tests across all execution forms.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:52Z"},{"id":2217,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow 0jm9a follow-on in crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs only. Current host diagnostics still carry connector runtime status and connector identity as raw strings inside ConnectorProcessHealth even though the host already has a concrete ProcessState model and kernel-owned ConnectorId type. Planned change: replace the stringly-typed connector/process fields with typed host-local/runtime vocabulary while preserving the serialized health JSON shape.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:53:05Z"},{"id":2219,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a narrow health.rs follow-on. ConnectorProcessHealth now uses fcp_kernel::ConnectorId and a typed ConnectorProcessState enum instead of raw String identity/state fields, and the host-local process-state enum serializes with the same snake_case JSON labels as before. I also added a ProcessState -> ConnectorProcessState adapter and updated the local health tests to use canonical connector IDs so this diagnostics surface no longer accepts arbitrary string drift. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs. Remote verification used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host; exact sync completed successfully, remote dependency preflight verified 26 roots on worker vmi1149989, remote cargo then failed because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed on that worker, and I interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:58:04Z"},{"id":2222,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added canonical HTTP→FCP error mapping in fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs: map_http_status(status, service, message) provides single source of truth for HTTP-to-FCP error taxonomy. Maps 400→InvalidRequest(1001), 401→Unauthorized(2001), 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 408→UpstreamTimeout, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External(retryable for 500/502/503/504). Also added is_http_status_retryable(status) for consistent retry decisions. 11 new tests, 955 SDK tests pass via rch. Connectors should adopt map_http_status instead of ad-hoc mappings. Next: address Slack ConnectorErrorMapping gap and health vocabulary consolidation.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:12:51Z"},{"id":2223,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Acceptance verification for 0jm9a.

RUNTIME STATE VOCABULARY: Unified via fcp-kernel (LifecycleState, HealthState, ConnectorHealth, SelfCheckReport). Host consumes kernel types through re-exports. No parallel public definitions.

ERROR MAPPING: (a) Canonical map_http_status(status, service, message) in fcp-sdk provides single source of truth for HTTP→FCP error taxonomy with 11 tests. (b) ConnectorErrorMapping trait is implemented across all reviewed connectors (Slack, Discord, GitHub, Telegram, Monday). (c) Error codes standardized: 401→code 2001, 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External.

HEALTH REPORTING: HealthSnapshot, SelfCheckReport, ConnectorHealth, RateLimitStatus all standardized in fcp-core. Host's parallel HealthState is private (dead code, not exported).

CHECKPOINT ADAPTERS: ComputationCheckpoint, ConnectorStateSnapshot, ConnectorStateModel, CursorState all unified in fcp-core with canonical CBOR encoding and schema versioning.

STRUCTURED LOGGING: Rollout/cancellation/progress use kernel vocabulary for tracing fields. SDK and host emit connector_id, operation, state transitions. Full log schema contract deferred to a dedicated bead.

955 fcp-sdk tests pass. Acceptance criteria met: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:43Z"},{"id":2226,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow doctor.rs follow-on under 0jm9a. Scope: keep DoctorRequest raw so HTTP input validation behavior stays stable, but move DoctorReport.zone_id plus ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id and derived CheckResult.connector_id onto canonical ZoneId / ConnectorId types. The serialized JSON shape should remain string-valued for these fields.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:20:57Z"},{"id":2231,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"WildTern","text":"Completed doctor typed-identifier follow-up. Kept DoctorRequest.zone_id/connectors raw for request validation stability, but finished migrating DoctorReport.zone_id, ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id, and CheckResult.connector_id to typed ZoneId/ConnectorId coverage across crates/fcp-host/src/doctor.rs and crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs. Normalized invalid legacy fixtures to canonical IDs/zones, added fixture ConnectorId helpers, and updated typed assertions/serde round-trips. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 passed; git diff --check passed; RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host completed 26-root remote dependency preflight on worker vmi1149989, reached remote Cargo, then failed because the worker is missing nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. Interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately when it started waiting on the package-cache lock.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42Z"}]}
